why: This is the actual PNG magic-signature comparison (png_sig_cmp != 0) that rejects files whose first 8 bytes don't match the standard PNG header, so an LLM could craft a file with a corrupted/non-standard signature to flip it.

Checks whether the raw chunk-length's top bit is set (buf[0] >= 0x80), gating whether a chunk length is treated as an invalid/oversized 31-bit value before any parsing proceeds, an easy field to mutate directly.

Dispatches based on whether the 4-byte chunk type name maps to a known/supported chunk handler versus falling through to png_handle_unknown, directly exercising PNG's chunk-type dispatch table via a crafted chunk type code.

Validates that the IHDR color_type/bit_depth combination is legal (e.g. rejecting palette images with bit_depth>8 or truecolor/alpha images with bit_depth<8), a core format-semantics check craftable by editing two IHDR bytes.
Checks whether the IHDR interlace_type byte is an unrecognized value (>= PNG_INTERLACE_LAST), a direct one-byte format field an LLM can set out of range to flip this branch.
Determines whether a PLTE chunk is meaningless/ignored because the IHDR color_type has no color component (grayscale), tying PLTE handling to a prior chunk's parsed field.
Enforces that a tRNS chunk for a palette-type image can only appear after a PLTE chunk has been seen, a cross-chunk ordering rule that's trivial to violate by reordering chunks in a crafted PNG.

Verifies the bKGD chunk's length equals the exact size required for the image's color_type (1, 2, or 6 bytes), a chunk-length-gates-parsing check an LLM can flip by mismatching bKGD length against IHDR color_type.

Checks that a palette-image bKGD chunk's index byte is within the bounds of the actual palette size from PLTE, a concrete out-of-range-index validation craftable by setting a bKGD index >= num_palette.
Cross-checks that an embedded iCCP ICC profile declaring an RGB data color space is only used with a color (RGB/RGBA/palette) IHDR color_type, rejecting an RGB profile paired with a grayscale image.
